docs.title: IODriver
docs.text:
Base driver for handling IO for different file formats/extensions

The primary purpose of this class is to enable reading source file from location specified in the source config. File can either be read locally or via sending a request to the hosting server.

docs.title: get_data() abstractmethod
docs.text: Read data based on config information

docs.title: create_collection()
docs.text:
Generate STAC Collection that includes all STAC Items from source dataframe

collection_cfg must be provided to constructor for this method to work

Returns:

Type Description pystac.Collection

STAC Collection that includes all generated STAC Items

docs.title: create_catalog()
docs.text:
Generate STAC Catalog that includes a STAC Collection containing all STAC Item generated from source dataframe

Both collection_cfg and catalog_cfg must be provided for this method to work

Returns:

Type Description pystac.Catalog

STAC Catalog

Generate STAC Collection and save to disk.

Contains parameters to pass to Collection constructor. Also contains other metadata

This config provides additional information that can not be derived from source file, which includes Stac Common Metadata and other descriptive information such as the id of the new entity

Contains parameters to pass to Item constructor.

This config provides additional information that can not be derived from source file, which includes Stac Common Metadata and other descriptive information such as the id of the new entity

docs.title: SourceConfig
docs.text:
Bases: StacItemConfig

Base source config that should be subclassed for different file extensions.

Source files contain raw spatial information (i.e. geotiff, shp, csv) from which some Stac metadata can be derived. SourceConfig describes:

  • The access mechanisms for the source file: stored on local disk, or hosted somewhere behind an api endpoint. If the source file must be accessed through an endpoint, users can provide additional HTTP information that forms the HTTP request to the host server.
  • Processing information that are unique for the source type. Users should inherit SourceConfig for file extensions currently unsupported.
  • Additional Stac Metadata from StacConfig

docs.title: location: str instance-attribute
docs.text: Asset's href. If local is not provided, will be used as endpoint for fetching data file, using other config parameters. If the file is on disk, it is acceptable to set location as ./path/to/source/file

docs.title: local: str | None = None class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text: Path to the source file on local disk. This is for reading in and processing local file. If local is not provided, HTTP methods and other parameters must be provided for reading in the file from location field

docs.title: extension: str | None = None class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text: Explicit file extension specification. If the file is stored behind an api endpoint, the field extension must be provided

docs.title: method: HTTPMethod | None = 'GET' class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text: HTTPMethod to acquire the file from location

docs.title: params: QueryParamTypes | None = None class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text: HTTP query params for getting file from location

docs.title: headers: HeaderTypes | None = None class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text: HTTP query headers for getting file from location

docs.title: cookies: CookieTypes | None = None class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text: HTTP query cookies for getting file from location

docs.title: content: RequestContent | None = None class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text: HTTP query body content for getting file from location

docs.title: data: RequestData | None = None class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text: HTTP query body content for getting file from location

docs.title: json_body: Any = None class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text: HTTP query body content for getting file from location

Raises:

if item is None

ValueError

if no type key is found in item

ValueError

if type is not GeometryCollection but no coordinates key is found in item

docs.title: extract_temporal_extent(items, collection=None)
docs.text:
Extract spatial extent for a collection from a list of items and collection config.

If temporal extent (start_datetime, end_datetime or datetime) is in collection, generate pystac.TemporalExtent from those fields. Otherwise, extract the fields from the provided items.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default items list[pystac.Item]

list of Items

required collection StacCollectionConfig | None

collection config, defaults to None

None

Returns:

Type Description pystac.TemporalExtent

extracted temporal extent

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

if a pystac.Item has neither datetime nor both start_datetime and end_datetime

Source config exteneded with CSVExtension fields

docs.title: read_csv(src_path, X_coord, Y_coord, T_coord=None, date_format='ISO8601', columns=None, groupby=None)
docs.text:
Read in csv from local disk

Users must provide at the bare minimum the location of the csv, and the names of the columns to be treated as the X and Y coordinates. By default, will read in all columns in the csv. If columns and groupby columns are provided, will selectively read specified columns together with the coordinate columns (X, Y, T).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default src_path str

path to csv file

required X_coord str

name of X field

required Y_coord str

name of Y field

required T_coord str | None

name of time field, defaults to None

None date_format str

format to pass to pandas to parse datetime, defaults to "ISO8601"

'ISO8601' columns list[str] | list[ColumnInfo] | None

band information, defaults to None

None groupby list[str] | None

list of fields that partition the points into groups, defaults to None

None

Returns:

Type Description PDFrameT

read dataframe

docs.title: calculate_temporal_extent(df=None, time_col=None, datetime=None, start_datetime=None, end_datetime=None)
docs.text:
Get temporal extent based on Stac specification.

Use start_datetime and end_datetime if provided. Otherwise, use datetime if provided. If the dataframe and time column are provided, the function will obtain the start datetime and end datetime from the dataframe.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default df FrameT | None

Provided dataframe.

None time_col str | None

Name of time column.

None datetime DateTimeT | None

Datetime.

None start_datetime DateTimeT | None

Start datetime.

None end_datetime DateTimeT | None

End datetime.

None

Returns:

Type Description TimeExtentT

Start datetime, end datetime.

docs.title: calculate_geometry(df)
docs.text:
Calculate the geometry from geopandas dataframe.

Returns a shapely.Point or shapely.MultiPoint depending on the number of unique points in the dataframe.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default df FrameT

source dataframe

required

Returns:

Type Description Point | MultiPoint

shapely geometry object

docs.title: group_df(df, prefix, groupby=None)
docs.text:
Partition dataframe into sub-dataframes based on fields in groupby. Each partition will be assigned an item name obtained from the collection name and field values.

For example, if the collection name is point_data and groupby = ["sites"] with values being ["A", "B"], the resulting item names will be point_data_site_A_item and point_data_site_B_item.

If groupby is not provided, return a single collection item which is the full dataframe.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default df FrameT

Source dataframe.

required prefix str

Prefix for each item ID.

required groupby Sequence[str] | None

Fields to partition the dataframe. Must be present in the original dataframe.

None

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, FrameT]

Mapping of group name to sub-dataframe.

docs.title: items_from_group_df(group_df, asset, epsg, T=None, datetime=None, start_datetime=None, end_datetime=None, properties=None)
docs.text:
Extract shapely.Point data from partitioned dataframe maps.

This function takes a group_df - i.e. dictionary of point group name and their dataframe (as obtained from stac_generator.csv.utils.group_df method) to generate a list of pystac.Item based on information from the dataframe.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default group_df dict[str, FrameT]

dictionary of point group and their source csv

required asset pystac.Asset

source data asset

required epsg int

epsg code of the source dataframe

required T str | None

name of the time column, defaults to None

None datetime DateTimeT | None

pystac datetime metadata, defaults to None

None start_datetime DateTimeT | None

pystac start_datetime metadata, defaults to None

None end_datetime DateTimeT | None

pystac end_datetime metadata, defaults to None

None properties dict[str, Any] | None

additional properties to be added to item

None

Returns:

Type Description list[pystac.Item]

list of generated stac items
